title: Token-Cost Failsafe
non_product_work: Resolve the bootstrap gap where Bandit lacks provider-pricing evidence, spend approval boundaries, soft budget bands, abnormal-run token-cost failsafes, trace-backed cost signals, and continuation decisions for paid, high-token, reviewer, scheduler, and long-running execution.
origin: The PRD research reviews identified token economics and multi-agent scheduler cost as unresolved risks, and the operator confirmed that budgets should act as generous abnormal-run failsafes rather than tight caps that force repeated failed attempts. `BANDIT-GAP-TOKEN-COST-FAILSAFE` is now the first queued bootstrap gap after `BANDIT-054` resolved Stage Capability Scope.
scope: Define a repo-native Token-Cost Failsafe policy artifact, expected first at `.bandit/policy/token-cost-failsafe.json`, that records soft budget bands, abnormal-run thresholds, provider-pricing evidence requirements, spend approval requirements, trace-backed cost-signal requirements, and continuation-decision requirements for paid, high-token, reviewer, scheduler, and long-running execution.

Define provider-pricing evidence fields for paid reviewer, paid model, or paid/high-token recurring execution: provider, model or profile, pricing source, captured date, effective date, freshness or expiry rule, expected per-run cost, spend class, and approval owner.

Define benchmark/evaluation spend boundaries for one-off paid reviewer or paid model calls before policy promotion, including current provider-pricing evidence, per-run approval or active spend-class approval, expected per-run cost, and explicit non-recurring routing disposition.

Define recurring paid reviewer or paid model route boundaries so recurring routes cannot become available policy without Provider Pricing Evidence, spend-class approval, a scoped promotion threshold, and the review-depth or stage capability profile they apply to.

Define soft budget bands as generous abnormal-run guardrails for normal deep reviews, scheduler activity, model calls, and long-running agent execution rather than tight caps that create duplicate failed attempts.

Define abnormal-run token-cost failsafe triggers for unexpected token/cost growth, repeated retries, repeated wake/no-op patterns, reviewer/runtime loops, missing trace cost signals, stale provider-pricing evidence, missing spend approval, and missing continuation decisions.
Define continuation-decision evidence for failsafe trips, including whether execution continued, rerouted, paused, stopped, or requires operator-owned cost/risk approval.

Bind token-cost failsafe requirements to Stage Capability Scope declarations, Agent Observability Trace signals, Event-Driven Wake Scheduler behavior, reviewer routing, and Stage 4/Stage 5 verification without making traces, projections, cockpit state, or pricing snapshots canonical workflow authority.

Add focused RED evidence before implementation for missing token-cost policy validation, missing provider-pricing evidence validation, missing spend approval validation, missing soft budget band validation, missing abnormal-run failsafe validation, missing trace-backed cost-signal validation, and missing continuation-decision validation.

Preserve the Permanent Test Ownership Boundary and Bootstrap Model-Family Separation: if Codex authors or materially edits Stage 2 RED tests, Stage 3 implementation is assigned to Claude through the bootstrap Process Adapter path, and the Stage 3 Writer has no authority over tests, test helpers, fixtures, RED evidence, or acceptance mappings.

Preserve the Claim Authority, Git Mutation Serializer, Worktree Bootstrap Contract, Event-Driven Wake Scheduler, Agent Observability Trace, Stage Capability Scope, Coordination Event Log Authority, Operator Fail-Closed Boundary, Input Quarantine Gate, Layered Risk Classification, Supply-Chain Gate, review-subject-hash, Evidence Freshness SLO queue, and repo-native source-of-truth boundaries.
Record CLEAN_CODE.md read evidence in Stage 1; CLEAN_CODE.md was read on 2026-05-30 before creating this brief, and clean-code compliance must be evaluated before landing.

Stage capability scope for this chore: Codex PM owns Stage 1 brief creation, Stage 2 RED evidence, routine technical routing, and context-artifact synchronization; if Codex authors or materially edits Stage 2 RED tests, Stage 3 implementation is assigned to Claude through the bootstrap Process Adapter path; the Stage 3 Writer has no authority to edit tests, test helpers, fixtures, RED evidence, or acceptance mappings; CodeRabbit and Local Qwen own Stage 4 review evidence; Landing Agent owns Stage 5 verdict/action evidence.

Future-work scope: this chore must not implement Evidence SLO policy, full scheduler execution, full worktree lifecycle work, claim lease creation or release, work-surface reservations, cockpit UI/server/API work, state-index persistence, PR/CI workflow, automatic merge/push/deploy behavior, product UAT approval, live reviewer routing changes, paid reviewer promotion, external service integration, installed global skill edits, dependency or lockfile changes, or unrelated Phase 8 work.
acceptance_criteria: The chore brief exists at docs/work/<ID>/brief.md and links to BANDIT-GAP-TOKEN-COST-FAILSAFE.

Stage 1 brief evidence records CLEAN_CODE.md read evidence, Stage Capability Scope requirements, Codex-owned technical decisions, source hierarchy, provider-pricing evidence boundary, benchmark/evaluation spend boundary, spend-class approval boundary, soft budget band boundary, abnormal-run token-cost failsafe boundary, trace-backed cost-signal boundary, and continuation-decision boundary.

Focused RED evidence proves Bandit currently lacks enforced Token-Cost Failsafe policy validation, provider-pricing evidence validation, spend approval validation, soft budget band validation, abnormal-run failsafe validation, trace-backed cost-signal validation, continuation-decision validation, and work-item/spec integration.
The Token-Cost Failsafe policy validates provider-pricing evidence fields for paid reviewer, paid model, and paid/high-token recurring routes.

Validation fails closed when paid reviewer, paid model, or paid/high-token recurring route policy lacks current provider-pricing evidence, spend-class approval, expected per-run cost, freshness or expiry rule, approval owner, or scoped route applicability.

Validation fails closed when one-off paid reviewer or paid model benchmark/evaluation spend lacks current provider-pricing evidence, expected per-run cost, per-run approval or active spend-class approval, and explicit non-recurring routing disposition.

Soft budget bands are modeled as generous abnormal-run failsafes that distinguish normal deep review variance from runaway retries, repeated wake/no-op loops, reviewer/runtime loops, and unexpected token or cost growth.
Continuation-decision evidence records whether execution continued, rerouted, paused, stopped, or requires operator-owned cost/risk approval after a failsafe trip.

Trace-backed token and cost signals are required where available but remain observability evidence only and cannot replace canonical workflow artifacts, approvals, landing evidence, UAT, or retrospective evidence.

Stage Capability Scope integration records when paid, high-token, reviewer, scheduler, or long-running stages need soft budget bands, provider-pricing evidence, spend approval, trace-backed cost signals, abnormal-run failsafes, and continuation decisions.
Stage 3 implementation scope preserves the Permanent Test Ownership Boundary and Bootstrap Model-Family Separation.
Stage 4 review evidence uses pre-PR CodeRabbit and Local Qwen at the current review subject hash unless honest provider refusal or bootstrap-gap evidence is recorded.

Layered risk-classification and supply-chain gate evidence are recorded before landing because this chore touches workflow policy validation, reviewer/model routing boundaries, cost approval boundaries, generated templates or schemas, and command routing.
Clean-code compliance is evaluated before landing; any accepted non-blocking concern becomes a tagged follow-up or explicit no-action decision.
BANDIT-GAP-TOKEN-COST-FAILSAFE is resolved or explicitly dispositioned in .bandit/bootstrap-gaps.json only after landing action and retrospective closeout evidence exist.

No Evidence SLO policy, full scheduler execution, full worktree lifecycle work, claim lease creation or release, work-surface reservation implementation, automatic merge/push/deploy behavior, product UAT approval, cockpit UI/server/API work, PR/CI workflow, live reviewer routing change, paid reviewer promotion, external service integration, installed global skill edit, dependency or lockfile change, or unrelated Phase 8 work is introduced.
verification_plan: Run focused Token-Cost Failsafe RED/GREEN tests for policy parsing, provider-pricing evidence fields, pricing freshness or expiry rules, expected per-run cost, spend class, approval owner, benchmark/evaluation spend boundaries, one-off non-recurring routing disposition, recurring route approval boundaries, soft budget bands, abnormal-run failsafe triggers, trace-backed cost signals, and continuation decisions.

Run focused tests proving soft budget bands distinguish normal deep review variance from runaway retries, repeated wake/no-op loops, reviewer/runtime loops, stale pricing evidence, missing approval, missing trace cost signals, and unexpected token or cost growth.
Run focused tests proving continuation decisions fail closed when a failsafe trip lacks continue, reroute, pause, stop, or operator-owned cost/risk escalation evidence.

Run focused tests proving trace-backed token and cost signals cannot satisfy or replace required workflow gates, work-item artifacts, provider-pricing evidence, spend approvals, UAT evidence, landing evidence, or retrospective evidence.

Run focused Stage Capability Scope integration tests proving paid, high-token, reviewer, scheduler, and long-running stages can declare token-cost failsafe requirements without creating paid routing policy by metadata alone.
Run node --test test/token-cost-failsafe.test.mjs.
Run node --test test/stage-capability-scope.test.mjs if Stage Capability Scope integration is touched.
Run node --test test/agent-observability.test.mjs if trace-backed cost-signal behavior is touched.
Run node --test test/event-driven-wake-scheduler.test.mjs if scheduler retry or wake/no-op budget behavior is touched.
Run node --test test/work-item-create.test.mjs if work-item spec validation, brief rendering, or template integration is touched.
Run node --test test/validate.test.mjs if repo validation behavior is touched.

Run npm test if implementation touches shared command routing, validators, artifact renderers, work item parsing, templates, bootstrap gaps, stage capability scope, agent observability traces, scheduler policy, risk classification, supply-chain gates, input quarantine, operator boundaries, claim authority, git mutation policy, cockpit status, coordination status, or policy validation beyond focused tests.
Run npm run typecheck.
Run npm run bandit -- validate.
Run npm run bandit -- gaps list.
Run node ./bin/bandit.mjs cockpit status --json.
Run node ./bin/bandit.mjs session-context current --json.
Run npm run bandit -- stage-capability-scope validate --json.
Run npm run bandit -- agent-observability validate --json if trace-backed cost-signal behavior is touched.
Run npm run bandit -- event-driven-wake-scheduler validate --json if scheduler behavior is touched.
Run npm run bandit -- risk-classification validate --json.

Run npm run bandit -- supply-chain-gate validate --json because token-cost policy can affect reviewer/model routing boundaries, generated schemas or templates, command routing, and paid execution surfaces.
Run npm run bandit -- input-quarantine validate --json.
Run npm run bandit -- operator-boundary validate --json.
Run node ./bin/bandit.mjs review-subject-hash <ID> for aggregate review evidence freshness.
Run npm run bandit -- coderabbit-review pre-pr <ID> --base origin/main before Stage 4 closeout, unless provider refusal evidence is recorded.
Run npm run bandit -- qwen-review <ID> before Stage 4 closeout.
Run npm run bandit -- land-check <ID> before landing.
Run git diff --check.
operator_input_status: No operator-owned input is required before creating this bootstrap-gap chore or writing RED evidence. Repo artifacts identify the queued gap, source artifacts, accepted token-cost failsafe boundary, current Stage Rubric requirements, Clean-Code authority, Stage Capability Scope boundary, Agent Observability Trace boundary, Event-Driven Wake Scheduler boundary, Operator Fail-Closed Boundary, Input Quarantine Gate, Layered Risk Classification, Supply-Chain Gate, and Codex PM boundary that token-cost validation mechanics are routine technical routing when repo evidence and policy are sufficient. Halt only if implementation would change product direction, UAT policy, workflow policy beyond enforcing the accepted token-cost failsafe gap, business tradeoffs, explicit cost/risk posture, provider-pricing approval, spend-class approval, paid reviewer promotion, recurring paid routing policy, external service setup, live routing, claim authority, worktree lifecycle authority, installed global skill contents, dependency or lockfile policy, merge/push/deploy authority, or broader workflow scope.
